review:
[h1] DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME OR MONEY. (300+ Hours / Level 200 Review) [/h1]

I have put [b]nearly 300 hours[/b] into this game, reached [b]Level 200[/b], and played countless squads matches with friends just messing around. Let me save you the time: [b]The only reason to play this game is if your friends force you to.[/b]

If you are looking for a functional, competitive Battle Royale, go play Fortnite, Apex Legends, or Warzone. Compared to them, PUBG is easily the worst BR on the market.

[hr][/hr]

[h2] 1. The Cheater Epidemic & The "Premium" Paywall [/h2]
Cheaters are absolutely everywhere. I’m not talking about Level 50 accounts that are just naturally good; I’m talking about blatant, unadulterated hacking.
• The page boasts about "weekly ban reports," but it does absolutely nothing to stop the influx.
• The game essentially forces you to buy [b]Premium+[/b] just to play Ranked, under the pathetic hope that you'll encounter fewer hackers there.
• I have no problem losing to a genuinely skilled player I'll gladly say "GG." But losing to script-users and wallhackers every single match is exhausting.

[h2] 2. A Completely Broken Death Cam [/h2]
The death cam is supposed to do two things: show you where you messed up, and help you verify if a player is using ESP, wallhacks, or recoil scripts.
• In this game, [b]the death cam is entirely useless.[/b]
• The development documentation itself has admitted that the death cam doesn't accurately replicate client-side recoil.
• What is the point of even having a death cam if it can't accurately show you how you died? Every other modern shooter can handle this, but PUBG can't.

[h2] 3. Archaic, Broken Movement [/h2]
Even the pro players admit the movement system is garbage. To quickly change directions (a 90° or 180° turn) while sprinting, you literally have to exploit and break the character animation by spamming the crouch button and standing back up. If a competitive shooter requires you to glitch the animation design just to turn around smoothly, it's a massive failure in development and animation design.

[h2] 4. Corporate Greed & Pathetic Rewards [/h2]
The monetization here is incredibly greedy. The rewards for playing are completely awful. It’s so bad that top pro players like TGLTN have resorted to changing their game language to Korean and altering back-end config files just to get decent rewards.
• If you want to use this game as a glorified, overpriced fashion show, be my guest.
• Yes, you can grind weekly challenges for BP coins to get free cosmetic loot, but the payout is [u]insulting[/u] for the time invested.

[hr][/hr]

[h3] Final Verdict [/h3]
The graphics are mediocre at best (you can run this on a potato mod and it changes nothing about the gameplay), the optimization is terrible, the movement is clunky, and the focus is clearly more on your wallet than fixing a broken game.

If you just want to mess around with friends on the weekend and don't care about dying to hackers, give it a try. But [b]do not seriously invest your time or hard-earned money into this game.[/b] It does not respect either.

review:
Initially I hated the game because of the recoil mechanics; it was truly hard to get into. I played when PUBG Lite was a thing on my old work laptop and it ran perfectly on integrated graphics - their old engine was great.

For some stupid reason the developers decided to move to an unoptimized engine instead of using their in-house version, and now in 2026 I run the game on a laptop with an RTX 2050 and can barely play without a gazillion optimization tweaks.

Performance rant aside, I think this game is amazing, it’s addictive. When I get a kill I feel exhilarated and amped up with adrenaline; it’s so good. Definitely more fun to play with friends.

I will say the game is not beginner-friendly: about 90% of the player base are veteran players, so the learning curve is steep. Other than that, it’s a great game if you enjoy competitive shooters and hate yourself.

review:
After all these years, PUBG remains the gold standard for the Battle Royale genre. It captures the tension, the adrenaline, and the tactical satisfaction that no other game in the market can replicate.

Why I recommend it:

Unmatched Tension: No other game makes your heart race like PUBG. From the silent stalks in the grass to the high-stakes final circles, every match tells a unique story.

Skill-Based Gameplay: There is no hand-holding here. The gunplay is realistic, demanding, and incredibly rewarding once you master the recoil patterns and bullet physics.

Tactical Depth: It’s not just about aiming; it’s about positioning, map knowledge, and making split-second decisions. The sheer variety of vehicles, weapons, and terrain makes every game feel fresh.

Constant Evolution: The developers have done a fantastic job keeping the game alive with new maps, weapon balances, and gameplay improvements that keep the community thriving.

If you enjoy a challenge and want to experience the true essence of survival, PUBG is the place to be. It is intense, gritty, and deeply addictive. Drop in, loot up, and fight for that Chicken Dinner!

Verdict: 10/10 – The king of Battle Royale.

review:
Game that popularized Battle Royal. It was a hardcore military shooter but now atmosphere has changed 
The game is still hard to master, but somehow you would enjoy first 100-200 hours of learning. First chicken dinner will taste absolutely delicious! 
The hardest thing to understand is spraying and ballistics.
Knowledge of the maps is really important too. The core of the game staying the same from the release - Loot,  Shoot, Regroup then repeat in any order. And that's cool! For the first 300 hours at least... Then you will remember every spot on the every map and every match will start to look the same 
I can recommend this game if you want to feel the original Battle Royal experience

review:
theres 2 versions of PUBG

the 2017-2020 era
the now era

the game now changed too much it's not the same PUBG we used to know / enjoy

4 addresses :

1- DEV team : 
make the animations more smooth, improve movements, make the game faster when moving around, doing successive actions, sometimes you can't even shoot the gun if you did another fast action before it the mouse click doesn't even register, optimize the game, under the map theres a LOT of useless mass objects that reduce performance.

2- Design team : 
less colorful skins, more combat / military skins, ask yourselves what made the 2017-2020 pubg great ? not the pink skins !!! it's the dark vibe and blendy enemies that surprise you whenever you get into a fight, it's PUBG not InZoi !!!!!

3- R&D team :
never add features no one asked for, LISTEND TO PLAYER FEEDBACK,these features ruined the game and changed it A LOT

self revive, self revive in solos ???!!!, blue ship system (pubg was great when you have 1 chance to win, that's what made it special, it was soooo intense, now it's not knowing i can die and respawn no sweat) => it's a BR game your are supposed to die, mediocre attachments that no one uses, unbalanced weapons still AUG is most picked for the last 3 years !!!!, you ruined DMRS too, the new execution move is so useless not needed and cheap it's not competitive i won't be letting my player exposed and shootable for that animation it doesn't fit pubg although it's okey in other games

4- PUBG Corp :
don't make decisions on your behalf only, listen to player feedback and needs, the players who are still playing pubg are the OLD 2017 players mostly, the loyal ones, it's true that the strategies you make now attract more players but these players won't stay, mostly try and leave, so make decisions that make the old loyal players stay and enjoy the game as it was because they make up the majority of PUBG players

PUBG is great it popularized and defined the modern genre of what BRs are today in terms of gameplay its great its the most grounded BR out there id say not crazy people killing you with ungodly creations its not oversaturated with weapons and gear that make you jump 60 feet in the air. if you want a just simple land loot fight BR this is it even though its not 2017 anymore its still a great game. Getting a chicken dinner in this game is a euphoric feeling in terms of weapons most of them are somewhat balanced but you definitely want to pick some up more than others. Sniping in the game is probably my favorite thing to do nothing like taking a guys head off 200 meters away and watching him try to crawl to the safety of his team just for you to put him down like a dog.

This game just like Day Z originated from Arma as well incase you were curious!

The game is highly popular in Asian countries like china and Vietnam so in terms of playing this game In NA the ranked gameplay takes around 10 minutes to find a match in ranked in NA server its also popular in the EU if you are American I consider playing this game as a patriotic Act taking the fight to the evil Red dragon Known as china. XD but in all seriousness this game is great and easy to learn so go out there and get yourself a chicken dinner on me.

Winner Winner Chicken Dinner

review:
This game has so much potential to be good but overall the circle rotations are disgusting. 

Most games encourage enemies to pull into a location to fight, this game I've seen players sit and have circle pull and pull and pull towards them. It's near impossible to get into a zone when there's players already camped out with snipers and you're running across a field. 

Some would say this is a skill issue, yeah maybe, maybe it's an issue with my zone predictions. But hands down, how many of you can say that you have zone pull towards you vs how many times you're running at an enemey who's already in zone and you've had to run 300+ metres. 

It's boring, the 4x4 maps are much better, but they get taken our of rotations forcing you to play 8x8. 

The store and D coins system is warped asf, lacks any kind of mercy system to prevent over spending and help players actually achieve their desired item. 

The games absolutely riddled with so many cheaters thast you can evidently see are using ESP wall hacks or Aimbot. Why do I keep playing? Because I enjoy the company of the people I play with, not for the enjoyment of the game. 

Mobile does it better. Change my mind.

review:
The battle royale part sucks and games like this are awful. Are you telling me people actually like sitting inside a house for 20-30 minutes? Also my squad keeps dying to what they report as "perfect accuracy." Take that as you will, I really don't care. Not to mention this game collabed with Dead by Daylight, which if you don't know is one of the worst games ever made and I pray for the day it's deleted forever. I don't get mad at video games, PUBG simply disappointed me because I wanted to play a game with my friends and what I got was a game too realistic. 

Because PUBG in real life would be awful to experience which the game tries to mimic, people camping houses near the dead zone of proning near a tree and blending in with the environment is the entire game. Every single gunfight is an unfair fight, there is a 0% chance where you will be in a scenario where the fighting ground is even and it all depends on your skill. Someone has the upper hand and wins instantly. So no, I'm not yelling at my monitor; I'm simply a bit disappointed. My reaction isn't AUGGGHADSDFHADJHSAD but more like "that was it?' It's a Christmas miracle that this game still has a playerbase as the core gameplay is awful, but understandable because it's addictive and free to play. 

I feel this is one of those games that trap you, where you think you're getting good but you're getting better at a game you hate. This is a reference to Dead by Daylight, PUBG gives me blank emotions whereas that game (which has a collab with this game) infuriates me. Where MINUTE 2 you know who is winning so what's the point of trying, top 3 things that got me mad? 3 is Getting locked outside of my house for hours. 2 is games collaborating with Dead by Daylight. and 1 is DbD itself and the fact people haven't rioted against BHVR is insulting considering the toxic fanbase it's created. The day I quit Dead by Daylight was the best day of my life, I remember my last game. I was a Ghostface and it was simply IMPOSSIBLE to win because if I didn't initiate in chase then gens would clear in 2 minutes and the ability where you could lift pallets was added, actually impossible. I was fuming, and I said proudly in that end game chat "I am uninstalling, I am never playing this crappy game again." And I never did. Which is the best decision I ever made, but also exposes an issue. 

Collabing with Dead by Daylight implies people will PLAY IT. And get addicted to a REALLY BAD game, it could get worse because they might DEFEND a bad game. And I'm assuming PUBG has that same "I THINK I'm improving" cycle, when in reality you're doing something incredibly easy. Only you're honing your skills at something the public knows is bad, Something you can only catch once you sink in a couple hundred hours, I don't want to fall for this trap again.


So why am I giving this game a POSITIVE review if I am practically saying this is the worst game ever made? Because I had fun. At the time of writing this review there is a Payday game mode, And I was playing that for hours. PUBG gave me one of the funnest nights I've had in a long time and it came from a LIMITED TIME GAMEMODE. Are you serious? THIS IS SO MUCH BETTER THAN THE ACTUAL BATTLE ROYALE. And this is why the Dead by Daylight discussion is crucial, people might try Dead by Daylight and get trapped playing a bad game and they won't be able to say anything because the devs of DbD are a bunch of power hungry pigs that will ban you with even the slightest amount of criticism and then resume to making the game worse. People that have spoken up about that game have been censored. Well that logic is about to get reversed, I had FUN playing Payday in PUBG. And this makes me want to uninstall PUBG and install Payday 2. So thank you PUBG, you don't need an anti-cheat or QoL. All you need to do is replace the entire game with a big green button that leads you to the Payday steam page. That is why this I am leaving a positive review, is this game bad... YES!!! Does it get better? No. But did it lead to something I wasn't opened to at first but ended up being incredibly fun? YES!!!!!

Someone has the upper hand and wins instantly. So no, I'm not yelling at my monitor I'm simply a bit disappointed. My reaction isn't AUGGGHADSDFHADJHSAD but more like "that was it?' It's a Christmas miracle that this game still has a playerbase as the core gameplay is awful, but understandable because it's addictive and free to play. I feel this is one of those games that trap you, where you think you're getting good but you're getting better at a game you hate. This is a reference to Dead by Daylight, PUBG gives me blank emotions whereas that game (which has a collab with this game) infuriates me. Where MINUTE 2 you know who is winning so what's the point of trying, top 3 things that got me mad? 3 is Getting locked outside of my house for hours. 2 is games collaborating with Dead by Daylight. and 1 is DbD itself and the fact people haven't rioted against BHVR is insulting considering the toxic fanbase it's created. The day I quit Dead by Daylight was the best day of my life, I remember my last game. I was a Ghostface and it was simply IMPOSSIBLE to win because if I didn't initiate in chase then gens would clear in 2 minutes and the ability where you could lift pallets was added, actually impossible. I was fuming, and I said proudly in that end game chat "I am uninstalling, I am never playing this crappy game again." And I never did. Which is the best decision I ever made, but also exposes an issue. Collabing with Dead by Daylight implies people will PLAY IT. And get addicted to a REALLY BAD game, it could get worse because they might DEFEND a bad game. And I'm assuming PUBG has that same "I THINK I'm improving" cycle, when in reality you're doing something incredibly easy. Only you're honing your skills at something the public knows is bad, Something you can only catch once you sink in a couple hundred hours, I don't want to fall for this trap again. So why am I giving this game a POSITIVE review if I am practically saying this is the worst game ever made? Because I had fun. At the time of writing this review there is a Payday game mode, And I was playing that for hours. PUBG gave me one of the funnest nights I've had in a long time and it came from a LIMITED TIME GAMEMODE. Are you serious? THIS IS SO MUCH BETTER THAN THE ACTUAL BATTLE ROYALE. And this is why the Dead by Daylight discussion is crucial, people might try Dead by Daylight and get trapped playing a bad game and they won't be able to say anything because the devs of DbD are a bunch of power hungry pigs that will ban you with even the slightest amount of criticism and then resume to making the game worse. People that have spoken up about that game have been censored. Well that logic is about to get reversed, I had FUN playing Payday in PUBG. And this makes me want to uninstall PUBG and install Payday 2. So thank you PUBG, you don't need an anti-cheat or QoL. All you need to do is replace the entire game with a big green button that leads you to the Payday steam page. That is why this I am leaving a positive review, is this game bad... YES!!! Does it get better? No. But did it lead to something I wasn't opened to at first but ended up being incredibly fun? YES!!!!!
